mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the GDP growth rate signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate within the last 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% decrease in the unemployment rate within the last 3 months signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate within the last 3 months, combined with a 0.2% decrease in the unemployment rate, signals a monetary easing cycle that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.5% in 3 months and UNRATE falls > 0.2% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the industrial production index within the last 3 months signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% increase in the consumer price index within the last 3 months, combined with a 0.5% increase in the GDP growth rate, signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% in 3 months and GDPC1 rises > 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate within the last 3 months, combined with a 0.2% increase in the average hourly earnings, signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5% in 3 months and PAYEMS rises > 0.2% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
